1: # Walkthrough - Volumetric Upgrades, Dev Commands, Credits & Achievements
2: 
3: We have successfully implemented all requested changes to make the game more volumetric, add developer console features, include game credits for `@sw1therland`, make the true ending attainable, and introduce a Steam-style achievement system.
4: 
5: ## Changes Made
6: 
7: ### 1. Game Credits for `@sw1therland`
8: - **Main Menu:** Added a subtitle `СОЗДАТЕЛЬ ИГРЫ: @sw1therland` directly under the subtitle.
9: - **"About the Game" Modal:** Inserted a line `РАЗРАБОТЧИК ИГРЫ: @sw1therland` right under the title.
10: - **Endings Credits:** Added a retro terminal-styled `#gameover-credits-container` inside the victory/endings screen, which rolls/shows `@sw1therland` as the creator and developer of the project, along with technology credits. This panel is displayed only during story endings (`ending_1` and `ending_2`) and is hidden during standard deaths.
11: 
12: ### 2. 3D Volumetric Door Handles
13: - Created `addVolumetricHandle(doorMesh)` in [app.js](file:///C:/Users/m3615/samosbor_game/app.js) to dynamically build 3D metallic handles on both sides of every door.
14: - Constructed each handle using volumetric circular valve wheels as shown in Photo 1:
15:   1. A metallic shaft/post cylinder protruding horizontally.
16:   2. A metallic torus (wheel) centered on the shaft.
17:   3. A horizontal cylinder spoke.
18:   4. A vertical cylinder spoke forming a clean cross shape in the wheel center.
19: - Attached handles as children to all spawned apartment doors in the `buildFloor()` generator, meaning they automatically rotate and swing in 3D space when a door is opened.
20: 
21: ### 3. Developer Console Command `hacker` & Testing Commands
22: - Added the `hacker` command in `executeDevCommand()` which:
23:   - Grants the hacker tool.
24:   - Sets battery charge to `999`.
25:   - Enables infinite battery charge (`window._infBatteries = true`), bypassing battery consumption for all subsequent hacks.
26: - Added `achievement` console utilities to help verify notifications:
27:   - `achievement unlock <id>`: Instantly unlocks an achievement.
28:   - `achievement progress <id> <amount>`: Increments progress of an achievement.
29:   - `achievement reset`: Resets all achievements to lock status.
30: 
31: ### 4. Steam-Style Achievement System
32: - Configured a local registry `ACHIEVEMENTS` tracking 7 unique milestones (including 2 new secret ones):
33:   1. `"gigahrush_butcher"`: **Мясник гигахруща** (Defeat or scare 50 crawlers/stairs monsters).
34:   2. `"self_taught_hacker"`: **Хакер-самоучка** (Hack 100 stairs doors).
35:   3. `"spendthrift"`: **Транжира** (Spend all ammo before the 1000th floor).
36:   4. `"survivor"`: **Выживший** (Descend below the 1000th floor).
37:   5. `"water_drinker"`: **Водохлёб** (Drink water 50 times).
38:   6. `"deceived"`: **Обманутый** (Unlock by reaching the bad ending `ending_2` / Тупиковая концовка). **[СЕКРЕТНОЕ]**
39:   7. `"awakened"`: **Пробуждённый и свободный** (Unlock by reaching the true wakeup ending `ending_1` / Истинная концовка). **[СЕКРЕТНОЕ]**
40: - **Secret Achievements Mechanics:** Locked secret achievements hide their title ("Секретное достижение") and description ("🔒 Пройдите игру, чтобы открыть подробности") in the Achievements list modal. They unlock normally with full info upon completion.
41: - Progress persists in the browser via `localStorage.getItem('samosbor_achievements')`.
42: - Styled a **Steam-style popup card** sliding from the bottom right with a neon green CRT outline.
43: - Developed a **chime sound synthesizer** using the Web Audio API (playing a classic arpeggio: C5 -> E5 -> G5 -> C6).
44: - Styled and coded a dedicated **Achievements modal** (ДОСТИЖЕНИЯ) in the main menu to view all achievements, complete with green glow cards for unlocked items, gray cards for locked ones, and real-time progress bars.
45: 
46: ### 5. Gameplay & Notes Ending Fix
47: - Increased the total number of collectible notes from 6 to 10.
48: - Updated the True Ending requirement: the player must now **collect all 10 notes AND read them** (open them in the inventory bag modal).
49: - **Trigger Wakeup Sequence:** As soon as the player closes the bag/notes modal (via `closeNotesModal()`) with all 10 notes collected and read:
50:   1. All player controls are disabled and current sounds (like alarms or monsters) stop.
51:   2. A **whiteout overlay** fades in, transitioning the screen to pure white over 4 seconds.
52:   3. The player wakes up, fading in a **blurry painting of a warm blue sky, green meadow grass, and a green tree** (generated procedurally at run-time using an HTML5 Canvas helper `drawProceduralNature()`).
53:   4. Peaceful, **ambient synthesizer chords** (C Major -> F Major -> G Major -> E Minor) loop in the background, synthesized via the Web Audio API (`playTrueEndingMusic()`).
54:   5. The Ending credits card fades in over 3 seconds, detailing the ending name, total floors descended, author name, and an enhanced, high-contrast, glowing green "Start new shift" restart button (`.glow-green-btn`).
55: - **High-Visibility Restart Button:** Replaced the low-contrast dark inline styling on the True Ending restart button with a custom `.glow-green-btn` styling (glowing green text and border on a dark `#121417` background) to ensure premium aesthetics and excellent legibility on the white credits card.
56: - **Waking Up Achievement Timing:** Moved `unlockAchievement("awakened")` to fire exactly when the credits modal fades in (1.5 seconds after the wakeup screen displays) instead of at the start of the whiteout, aligning the unlock notification with the card visual.
57: - **Main Menu Wakeup Theme (Post-True Ending):** If the player has unlocked the true ending:
58:   1. Hides the yellow-black warning hazard stripes in the main menu to make the menu feel cleaner, lighter, and more open.
59:   2. Changes the title text color from glowing neon red (`glow-red`) to a serene green (`glow-green`) with a custom shadow.
60:   3. Updates the developer console/terminal simulator text block in the main menu to show a peaceful, story-oriented decompilation report (*"> ДЕКОМПИЛЯЦИЯ КОШМАРА... ОК. > ВОЗДУХ: ЧИСТЫЙ..."*).
61:   4. Appends a dynamic HTML5 Canvas background (`#menu-nature-canvas`) running a loop: rendering a dreamy sky, drifting clouds, a tree that sways in the wind, a grass hill, and glowing pollen particles drifting across the screen (softly blurred by 10px and rendered at 55% opacity for perfect readability).
62:   5. Plays the peaceful true ending music in the main menu. To respect browser autoplay policies, this starts automatically upon the user's first interaction (click or keypress) in the main menu.
63:   6. Automatically disables this theme and clean ups the canvas when starting a new game (starts standard atmosphere/heartbeat) and restores it when returning to the menu or resetting achievements.
64: - Added creepy, immersive storylines to the notes:
65:   - **Note 4 (Description of quarters):** Describes the Liquidator's exact room ( койка, ржавый вентиль, алюминиевый чайник) and leaves the Liquidator wondering: *"Откуда автор этой записки знает, как выглядит моя комната?"*.
66:   - **Note 5 (Illusion check):** Dynamically inserts the player's current floor using the `[FLOOR]` tag (e.g., *"Ты сейчас находишься на 1120 этаже... ты спишь"*), reminding the player that a descent in a concrete box without food/rest is a dream.
67:   - **Note 7 (Wake up loop):** Renders a fullscreen text repeating *"ПРОСНИСЬ ПРОСНИСЬ ПРОСНИСЬ..."* styled in glowing red neon monospace.
68: - **Space Melee Kick Self-Defense:**
69:   * Added a **Kick** action (`performPlayerKick()`) mapped to the `Space` key on desktop (and described in instructions).
70:   * If the corridor crawler gets within 3.5 meters of the player, pressing `Space` kicks it, pushing it back by 5.0 meters, dealing 1 damage, and playing a custom Web Audio API hit sound (and a swoosh sound if swinging at empty air).
71:   * A custom hand/weapon kick visual recoil animation is played. Kicking the monster to death counts towards the "Butcher" achievement.
72: - **Predetermined Loot & Progression Gate (Forced Looting):**
73:   * Shifted the loot system from roll-on-the-fly to a predetermined scheme. Each apartment generated on a floor is pre-assigned a specific loot item (`door.loot`) taking room types into account.
74:   * Predetermined the **Hacker Tool** to spawn in the first room on floor **1320**.
75:   * Forced a **locked stairs door** on floor **1320** (which leads to floor 1319). Because it is locked, the player cannot descend until they search the rooms on floor 1320, find the hacker tool, and bypass the gate. This ensures they always obtain it.
76: - **Rendering & Memory Disposal Optimizations:**
77:   * Implemented an early return check at the top of the Three.js `animate3D()` loop. If the game interface (`#main-interface`) is inactive (e.g. in the menus or credits), it skips all physics, beacon animations, raycasting, and WebGL rendering, saving huge CPU/GPU cycles.
78:   * Added a recursive `disposeHierarchy` helper and `removeAndDispose` wrapper that disposes of Three.js materials, geometries, and array assets when rebuilding floor scenes, preventing severe WebGL memory leaks during long runs.
79: - **Notes UI Counter Limit:** Corrected the bag UI button markup in `index.html` to display `/10` notes instead of `/6` notes to match the new 10-notes requirement.
80: - Removed the restriction `state.floor >= 700` from `distributeLoot()` note spawns so notes can spawn on any floor, ensuring players don't get locked out of the True Notes Ending if they miss a note above floor 700.
81: - Updated the developer console:
82:   - Running `give notes` awards all 10 notes (allowing manual reading testing).
83:   - Running `give notes read` awards all 10 notes and marks them as read instantly (to bypass manual reading for speed runs/instant testing).

13:

17:   - **Noon**: Bright blue sky (`#7ec0ee`, `#e0f6ff`).
18:   - **Sunset**: Orange/purple sky.
19:   - **Night**: Dark blue/black sky with stars.

28:

Line 3801: source=MODEL, len=2017
Content:
Проблема найдена и решена. 

В коде был баг: когда вы заходили в комнату для обыска, игра "забывала", какую дверь вы открыли (потому что вы переставали на неё смотреть). Из-за этого функция выдачи лута ломалась и ничего не писала. Я привязал функцию обыска к конкретной открытой двери, так что теперь при обыске комнаты вам **в 100% случаев будет писаться в консоль лог:** либо что вы нашли предмет, либо "Комната пуста, вы ничего не нашли".

Я также скорректировал шанс появления вещей — теперь в комнатах генерируется какой-либо предмет ровно с шансом **60%** (а в 40% случаев будет выпадать "пустота").

И по поводу твоего раннего запроса "вынуждать находить взломщик на первом этаже" — это правило уже заложено в игру. Лестничная дверь на самом первом этаже (1320-м) всегда генерируется **запертой намертво**, поэтому игрок не сможет пройти дальше, пока не пойдет обыскивать соседние квартиры и не выбьет взломщик (он специально спавнится на этом этаже).

Можешь заходить и проверять процесс лута

2275: function getStepsY(x, y, z) {

**Evaluating Wall Interactions**

**Analyzing Camera Near Plane**

However, a reduced near plane of 0.02 completely solves any wall-clipping issues. This confirms that decreasing the `camera.near` is a standard fix. 32:     if (!door) return "";
33:     if (door.type === 'apartment') {
34:         const num = door.number || 101;
35:         if (door.roomType === 'armory') {
36:             return `Пост Ликвидаторов №${num}`;
37:         } else if (door.roomType === 'contaminated') {
38:             return `Квартира ${num} (РАДИАЦИЯ)`;
39:         } else if (door.roomType === 'nest') {
40:             return `Техническая секция ${num} (ГНЕЗДО)`;
41:         } else {
42:             return `Квартира ${num}`;
43:         }
44:     } else if (door.type === 'transition') {
45:         return `Переход ${door.sector || 'А'}-${door.sectorNum || 1}`;
46:     } else if (door.type === 'monster') {
47:         const keys = ['room_warehouse', 'room_archive', 'room_compressor', 'room_control', 'room_ventilation'];

55: }

1: # Implementation Plan - Settings Modal & Multi-Language Translation (i18n)
2: 
3: This plan details the design and implementation for adding a unified **Settings** ("Настройки") tab/modal in both the main menu and the pause menu to configure controls and language, with complete support for six languages: Russian (`ru`), English (`en`), Chinese (`zh`), German (`de`), Italian (`it`), and Spanish (`es`).
4: 
5: ## User Review Required
6: 
7: > [!NOTE]
8: > The language select and controls rebinding will be combined into a single visual panel `#settings-modal`. Selecting a language will immediately refresh all HUD displays, dynamically generated text blocks (such as achievements list, note contents, game over screens), and in-game log feed without requiring a restart.
9: 
10: ## Proposed Changes
11: 
12: We will modify [app.js](file:///C:/Users/m3615/samosbor_game/app.js) and [index.html](file:///C:/Users/m3615/samosbor_game/index.html).
13: 
14: ---
15:

20:   - Main menu title, subtitles, status log lines, and buttons (`btn-start-game`, `btn-open-about`, `btn-open-achievements`, `btn-open-settings`).
21:   - HUD overlay labels (`HUD-FLOOR`, `HUD-HEALTH`, `HUD-WATER`, `HUD-AMMO`, `HUD-FILTER`, `HUD-STAMINA`).

technical sections in the active language.
54: - **Wire UI Modal Events:**
55:   - Replace older modal triggers (`#btn-open-rebind`, `#btn-pause-rebind`, `#rebind-modal`, `#rebind-close`) with Settings equivalents (`#btn-open-settings`, `#btn-pause-settings`, `#settings-modal`, `#settings-close`).
56:   - Add event listener to `#settings-language-select`:

1390: function t(key, ...args) {
1391:     const lang = (state && state.language) || 'ru';
1392:     const db = LANGUAGES[lang] || LANGUAGES['ru'];
1393:     let val = db[key];
1394:     if (val === undefined) {
1395:         val = LANGUAGES['ru'][key];
1396:     }
1397:     if (val === undefined) {
1398:         return key;
1399:     }
1400:     if (args.length > 0) {
1401:         for (let i = 0; i < args.length; i++) {
1402:             val = val.replace(`{${i}}`, args[i]);
1403:         }
1404:     }
1405:     return val;
1406: }
1407: 
1408: function getLocalizedDoorName(door) {
1409:     if (!door) return "";
1410:     if (door.type === 'apartment') {
1411:         const num = door.number || 101;
1412:         if (door.roomType === 'armory') {
1413:             return t('door_armory', num);
1414:         } else if (door.roomType === 'contaminated') {
1415:             return t('door_contaminated', num);
1416:         } else if (door.roomType === 'nest') {
1417:             return t('door_nest', num);
1418:         } else {

11: 
12: ### 2. 3D Volumetric Door Handles
13: - Created `addVolumetricHandle(doorMesh)` in [app.js](file:///C:/Users/m3615/samosbor_game/app.js) to dynamically build 3D metallic handles on both sides of every door.
14: - Constructed each handle using volumetric circular valve wheels as shown in Photo 1:
15:   1. A metallic shaft/post cylinder protruding horizontally.
16:   2. A metallic torus (wheel) centered on the shaft.
17:   3. A horizontal cylinder spoke.
18:   4. A vertical cylinder spoke forming a clean cross shape in the wheel center.
19: - Attached handles as children to all spawned apartment doors in the `buildFloor()` generator, meaning they automatically rotate and swing in 3D space when a door is opened.
20:

22: - Added the `hacker` command in `executeDevCommand()` which:
23:   - Grants the hacker tool.
24:   - Sets battery charge to `999`.
25:   - Enables infinite battery charge (`window._infBatteries = true`), bypassing battery consumption for all subsequent hacks.
26: - Added `achievement` console utilities to help verify notifications:
27:   - `achievement unlock <id>`: Instantly unlocks an achievement.
28:   - `achievement progress <id> <amount>`: Increments progress of an achievement.
29:   - `achievement reset`: Resets all achievements to lock status.
30: 
31: ### 4. Steam-Style Achievement System
32: - Configured a local registry `ACHIEVEMENTS` tracking 7 unique milestones (including 2 new secret ones):
33:   1. `"gigahrush_butcher"`: **Мясник гигахруща** (Defeat or scare 50 crawlers/stairs monsters).
34:   2. `"self_taught_hacker"`: **Хакер-самоучка** (Hack 100 stairs doors).
35:   3. `"spendthrift"`: **Транжира** (Spend all ammo before the 1000th floor).
36:   4. `"survivor"`: **Выживший** (Descend below the 1000th floor).
37:   5. `"water_drinker"`: **Водохлёб** (Drink water 50 times).
38:   6. `"deceived"`: **Обманутый** (Unlock by reaching the bad ending `ending_2` / Тупиковая концовка). **[СЕКРЕТНОЕ]**
39:   7. `"awakened"`: **Пробуждённый и свободный** (Unlock by reaching the true wakeup ending `ending_1` / Истинная концовка). **[СЕКРЕТНОЕ]**
40: - **Secret Achievements Mechanics:** Locked secret achievements hide their title ("Секретное достижение") and description ("🔒 Пройдите игру, чтобы открыть подробности") in the Achievements list modal. They unlock normally with full info upon completion.
41: - Progress persists in the browser via `localStorage.getItem('samosbor_achievements')`.
42: - Styled a **Steam-style popup card** sliding from the bottom right with a neon green CRT outline.
43: - Developed a **chime sound synthesizer** using the Web Audio API (playing a classic arpeggio: C5 -> E5 -> G5 -> C6).
44: - Styled and coded a dedicated **Achievements modal** (ДОСТИЖЕНИЯ) in the main menu to view all achievements, complete with green glow cards for unlocked items, gray cards for locked ones, and real-time progress bars.
45: 
46: ### 5. Gameplay & Notes Ending Fix
47: - Increased the total number of collectible notes from 6 to 10.
48: - Updated the True Ending requirement: the player must now **collect all 10 notes AND read them** (open them in the inventory bag modal).
49: - **Trigger Wakeup Sequence:** As soon as the player closes the bag/notes modal (via `closeNotesModal()`) with all 10 notes collected and read:
50:   1. All player controls are disabled and current sounds (like alarms or monsters) stop.
51:   2. A **whiteout overlay** fades in, transitioning the screen to pure white over 4 seconds.
52:   3. The player wakes up, fading in a **blurry painting of a warm blue sky, green meadow grass, and a green tree** (generated procedurally at run-time using an HTML5 Canvas helper `drawProceduralNature()`).
53:   4. Peaceful, **ambient synthesizer chords** (C Major -> F Major -> G Major -> E Minor) loop in the background, synthesized via the Web Audio API (`playTrueEndingMusic()`).
54:   5. The Ending credits card fades in over 3 seconds, detailing the ending name, total floors descended, author name, and an enhanced, high-contrast, glowing green "Start new shift" restart button (`.glow-green-btn`).

57: - **Main Menu Wakeup Theme (Post-True Ending):** If the player has unlocked the true ending:
58:   1. Hides the yellow-black warning hazard stripes in the main menu to make the menu feel cleaner, lighter, and more open.
59:   2. Changes the title text color from glowing neon red (`glow-red`) to a serene green (`glow-green`) with a custom shadow.
60:   3. Updates the developer console/terminal simulator text block in the main menu to show a peaceful, story-oriented decompilation report (*"> ДЕКОМПИЛЯЦИЯ КОШМАРА... ОК. > ВОЗДУХ: ЧИСТЫЙ..."*).
61:   4. 62:   5. Plays the peaceful true ending music in the main menu. To respect browser autoplay policies, this starts automatically upon the user's first interaction (click or keypress) in the main menu.
63:   6. Automatically disables this theme and clean ups the canvas when starting a new game (starts standard atmosphere/heartbeat) and restores it when returning to the menu or resetting achievements.
64: - Added creepy, immersive storylines to the notes:
65:   - **Note 4 (Description of quarters):** Describes the Liquidator's exact room ( койка, ржавый вентиль, алюминиевый чайник) and leaves the Liquidator wondering: *"Откуда автор этой записки знает, как выглядит моя комната?"*.
66:   - **Note 5 (Illusion check):** Dynamically inserts the player's current floor using the `[FLOOR]` tag (e.g., *"Ты сейчас находишься на 1120 этаже... ты спишь"*), reminding the player that a descent in a concrete box without food/rest is a dream.
67:   - **Note 7 (Wake up loop):** Renders a fullscreen text repeating *"ПРОСНИСЬ ПРОСНИСЬ ПРОСНИСЬ..."* styled in glowing red neon monospace.
68: - **Space Melee Kick Self-Defense:**
69:   * Added a **Kick** action (`performPlayerKick()`) mapped to the `Space` key on desktop (and described in instructions).
70:   * If the corridor crawler gets within 3.5 meters of the player, pressing `Space` kicks it, pushing it back by 5.0 meters, dealing 1 damage, and playing a custom Web Audio API hit sound (and a swoosh sound if swinging at empty air).
71:   * A custom hand/weapon kick visual recoil animation is played. Kicking the monster to death counts towards the "Butcher" achievement.
72: - **Predetermined Loot & Progression Gate (Forced Looting):**
73:   * Shifted the loot system from roll-on-the-fly to a predetermined scheme. Each apartment generated on a floor is pre-assigned a specific loot item (`door.loot`) taking room types into account.
74:   * Predetermined the **Hacker Tool** to spawn in the first room on floor **1320**.
75:   * Forced a **locked stairs door** on floor **1320** (which leads to floor 1319). Because it is locked, the player cannot descend until they search the rooms on floor 1320, find the hacker tool, and bypass the gate. This ensures they always obtain it.
76: - **Rendering & Memory Disposal Optimizations:**
77:   * Implemented an early return check at the top of the Three.js `animate3D()` loop. If the game interface (`#main-interface`) is inactive (e.g. in the menus or credits), it skips all physics, beacon animations, raycasting, and WebGL rendering, saving huge CPU/GPU cycles.
78:   * Added a recursive `disposeHierarchy` helper and `removeAndDispose` wrapper that disposes of Three.js materials, geometries, and array assets when rebuilding floor scenes, preventing severe WebGL memory leaks during long runs.
79: - **Notes UI Counter Limit:** Corrected the bag UI button markup in `index.html` to display `/10` notes instead of `/6` notes to match the new 10-notes requirement.
80: - Removed the restriction `state.floor >= 700` from `distributeLoot()` note spawns so notes can spawn on any floor, ensuring players don't get locked out of the True Notes Ending if they miss a note above floor 700.
81: - Updated the developer console:
82:   - Running `give notes` awards all 10 notes (allowing manual reading testing).
83:   - Running `give notes read` awards all 10 notes and marks them as read instantly (to bypass manual reading for speed runs/instant testing).

function loadAchievements() {
    const saved = localStorage.getItem('samosbor_achievements');
    if (saved) {
        try {
            const parsed = JSON.parse(saved);
            for (let id in ACHIEVEMENTS) {
                if (parsed[id]) {
                    ACHIEVEMENTS[id].unlocked = !!parsed[id].unlocked;
                    ACHIEVEMENTS[id].progress = parseInt(parsed[id].progress) || 0;
                }
            }
        } catch(e) {
            console.error("Failed to load achievements", e);
        }
    }
}

				function togglePauseGame(pause = !isGamePaused) {
    const splash = document.getElementById('splash-screen');
    const goScreen = document.getElementById('gameover-screen');
    if (splash.classList.contains('screen-active') || goScreen.classList.contains('screen-active')) {
        return;
    }
    
    isGamePaused = pause;
    const pauseScreen = document.getElementById('pause-screen');
    if (isGamePaused) {
        pauseScreen.className = 'panel screen-active modal-backdrop';
        if (document.pointerLockElement) {
            document.exitPointerLock();
        }
        Object.keys(keys).forEach(k => keys[k] = false);
    } else {
        pauseScreen.className = 'panel screen-inactive modal-backdrop';
        const canvasHolder = document.getElementById('canvas-holder');
        if (canvasHolder && !state.isSearching) {
            canvasHolder.requestPointerLock();
        }
    }
}

31: function getLocalizedDoorName(door) {
32:     if (!door) return "";
33:     if (door.type === 'apartment') {
34:         const num = door.number || 101;
35:         if (door.roomType === 'armory') {
36:             return `Пост Ликвидаторов №${num}`;
37:         } else if (door.roomType === 'contaminated') {
38:             return `Квартира ${num} (РАДИАЦИЯ)`;
39:         } else if (door.roomType === 'nest') {
40:             return `Техническая секция ${num} (ГНЕЗДО)`;
41:         } else {
42:             return `Квартира ${num}`;
43:         }
44:     } else if (door.type === 'transition') {
45:         return `Переход ${door.sector || 'А'}-${door.sectorNum || 1}`;
46:     } else if (door.type === 'monster') {
47:         const keys = ['room_warehouse', 'room_archive', 'room_compressor', 'room_control', 'room_ventilation'];
48:         const key = keys[door.monsterRoomIndex] || 'room_warehouse';
49:         return t(key);
50:     } else if (door.type === 'empty') {
51:         const idx = door.id !== undefined ? (door.id + 1) : 1;
52:         return `Дверь ${idx}`;
53:     }
54:     return door.name || "";
55: }

140:             const parsed = JSON.parse(saved);
141:             for (let id in ACHIEVEMENTS) {
142:                 if (parsed[id]) {
143:                     ACHIEVEMENTS[id].unlocked = !!parsed[id].unlocked;
144:                     ACHIEVEMENTS[id].progress = parseInt(parsed[id].progress) || 0;
145:                 }
146:             }
147:         } catch(e) {
148:             console.error("Failed to load achievements", e);
149:         }
150:     }
151: }
152: 
153: function saveAchievements() {
154:     try {
155:         const data = {};
156:         for (let id in ACHIEVEMENTS) {
157:             data[id] = {
158:                 unlocked: ACHIEVEMENTS[id].unlocked,
159:                 progress: ACHIEVEMENTS[id].progress
160:             };
161:         }
162:         localStorage.setItem('samosbor_achievements', JSON.stringify(data));
163:     } catch(e) {
164:         console.error("Failed to save achievements", e);
165:     }
166: }
167: 
168: function progressAchievement(id, amount = 1) {
169:     const ach = ACHIEVEMENTS[id];
170:     if (!ach || ach.unlocked) return;
171:     
172:     ach.progress = Math.min(ach.target, ach.progress + amount);
173:     saveAchievements();
174:     updateAchievementsModalUI();
175:     
176:     if (ach.progress >= ach.target) {
177:         unlockAchievement(id);
178:     }
179: }
180: 
181: function unlockAchievement(id) {
182:     const ach = ACHIEVEMENTS[id];
183:     if (!ach || ach.unlocked) return;
184:     
185:     ach.unlocked = true;
186:     ach.progress = ach.target;
187:     saveAchievements();
188:     updateAchievementsModalUI();
189:     
190:     // Play chime sound
191:     playAchievementUnlockSound();
192:     
193:     // Display visual Steam-style popup
194:     const popup = document.getElementById('achievement-popup');
195:     const titleEl = document.getElementById('achievement-popup-title');
196:     if (popup && titleEl) {
197:         titleEl.innerText = ach.title;
198:         popup.classList.remove('achievement-popup-hidden');
199:         popup.classList.add('achievement-popup-visible');
200:         
201:         if (window._achievementPopupTimeout) {
202:             clearTimeout(window._achievementPopupTimeout);
203:         }
204:         window._achievementPopupTimeout = setTimeout(() => {
205:             popup.classList.remove('achievement-popup-visible');
206:             popup.classList.add('achievement-popup-hidden');
207:         }, 4000);
208:     }
209:     
210:     logToConsole(`🏆 ДОСТИЖЕНИЕ ПОЛУЧЕНО: "${ach.title}"!`, "loot");
211: }
212: 
213: function playAchievementUnlockSound() {
214:     initAudio();
215:     if (!audioCtx) return;
216:     try {
217:         const now = audioCtx.currentTime;
218:         const osc1 = audioCtx.createOscillator();
219:         const gainNode = audioCtx.createGain();
220:         
221:         osc1.type = 'sine';
222:         // Retro C5-E5-G5-C6 major arpeggio chime
223:         osc1.frequency.setValueAtTime(523.25, now);
224:         osc1.frequency.setValueAtTime(659.25, now + 0.1);
225:         osc1.frequency.setValueAtTime(783.99, now + 0.2);
226:         osc1.frequency.setValueAtTime(1046.50, now + 0.3);
227:         
228:         gainNode.gain.setValueAtTime(0.001, now);
229:         gainNode.gain.linearRampToValueAtTime(0.18, now + 0.05);
230:         gainNode.gain.exponentialRampToValueAtTime(0.001, now + 0.7);
231:         
232:         osc1.connect(gainNode);
233:         gainNode.connect(audioCtx.destination);
234:         
235:         osc1.start(now);
236:         osc1.stop(now + 0.7);
237:     } catch(e) {
238:         console.error("Audio chime error:", e);
239:     }
240: }
241: 
242: function updateAchievementsModalUI() {
243:     const container = document.getElementById('achievements-list-container');
244:     if (!container) return;
245:     
246:     container.innerHTML = '';
247:     for (let id in ACHIEVEMENTS) {
248:         const ach = ACHIEVEMENTS[id];
249:         const card = document.createElement('div');
250:         card.className = `achievement-card ${ach.unlocked ? 'unlocked' : 'locked'}`;
251:         
252:         const isSecretLocked = ach.secret && !ach.unlocked;
253:         const displayTitle = isSecretLocked ? "Секретное достижение" : ach.title;
254:         const displayDesc = isSecretLocked ? "🔒 Пройдите игру, чтобы открыть подробности" : ach.description;
255:         
256:         let progressHTML = '';
257:         if (ach.target > 1 && !isSecretLocked) {
258:             const pct = (ach.progress / ach.target) * 100;
259:             progressHTML = `
260:                 <div class="achievement-card-progress-container">
261:                     <div class="achievement-card-progress-bar" style="width: ${pct}%;"></div>
262:                 </div>
263:                 <div class="achievement-card-progress-text">${ach.progress} / ${ach.target}</div>
264:             `;
265:         }
266:         
267:         card.innerHTML = `
268:             <div class="achievement-card-icon">
269:                 ${ach.unlocked ? '🏆' : '🔒'}
270:             </div>
271:             <div class="achievement-card-info">
272:                 <div class="achievement-card-title">${displayTitle}</div>
273:                 <div class="achievement-card-desc">${displayDesc}</div>
274:                 ${progressHTML}
275:             </div>
276:             <div class="achievement-card-status">
277:                 ${ach.unlocked ? 'ПОЛУЧЕНО' : 'ЗАБЛОКИРОВАНО'}
278:             </div>
279:         `;
280:         container.appendChild(card);
281:     }
282: }
283: 
284: function openAchievementsModal() {
285:     updateAchievementsModalUI();
286:     const modal = document.getElementById('achievements-modal');
287:     if (modal) modal.classList.remove('modal-hidden');
288: }
289: 
290: function closeAchievementsModal() {
291:     const modal = document.getElementById('achievements-modal');
292:     if (modal) modal.classList.add('modal-hidden');
293: }

326:         if (!this.floorsData) {
327:             this.floorsData = {};
328:         }
329:         this.floorsData[this.floor] = { doors: val };
330:     },

335:     location: 'hallway', // 'hallway', 'room', 'transition'
336:     samosborSafe: false, 
337:     searchProgress: 0,
338:     isSearching: false,
339:

342:     stairsMonsterTimeLeft: 0,

350:     batteries: 0,

398: function loadKeyBindings() {
399:     try {
400:         const saved = localStorage.getItem('samosbor_keybindings');
401:         if (saved) {
402:             state.keyBindings = JSON.parse(saved);
403:             for (let k in DEFAULT_KEY_BINDINGS) {
404:                 if (!state.keyBindings[k]) {
405:                     state.keyBindings[k] = DEFAULT_KEY_BINDINGS[k];
406:                 }
407:             }
408:             return;
409:         }
410:     } catch (e) {
411:         console.warn("Failed to load keybindings:", e);
412:     }
413:     state.keyBindings = Object.assign({}, DEFAULT_KEY_BINDINGS);
414: }
415: 
416: function saveKeyBindings() {
417:     try {
418:         localStorage.setItem('samosbor_keybindings', JSON.stringify(state.keyBindings));
419:     } catch (e) {
420:         console.error("Failed to save keybindings:", e);
421:     }
422: }
423: 
424: // --- УПРАВЛЕНИЕ ЛЕСТНИЧНЫМИ ДВЕРЯМИ ---
425: function getOrGenerateStairsDoor(floorNum) {
426:     if (!state.stairsDoors) {
427:         state.stairsDoors = {};
428:     }
429:     if (state.stairsDoors[floorNum] !== undefined) {
430:         return state.stairsDoors[floorNum];
431:     }
432:     if (floorNum === START_FLOOR) {
433:         state.stairsDoors[floorNum] = { opened: true };
434:     } else if (floorNum === 1) {
435:         state.stairsDoors[floorNum] = { opened: false };
436:     } else if (floorNum === 1320) {
437:         // Guaranteed locked to force player to loot apartments on floor 1320 for hacker tool
438:         state.stairsDoors[floorNum] = { opened: false };
439:     } else {
440:         // Если у игрока ещё нет взломщика гермодверей, затворы не могут быть закрытыми
441:         if (!state.hasHackerTool) {
442:             state.stairsDoors[floorNum] = { opened: true };
443:         } else {
444:             // 40% chance of being closed (needs hacker tool)
445:             state.stairsDoors[floorNum] = { opened: Math.random() >= 0.4 };
446:         }
447:     }
448:     return state.stairsDoors[floorNum];
449: }
450: let pointerLocked = false;
451: let footstepTimeAccumulator = 0; // Накопитель времени для звуков шагов
452: const playerSpeed = 4.0;
453: const sensitivity = 0.0025;
454: 
455: // Мобильное управление движением (флаги виртуального D-pad)
456: let mvUp = false, mvDown = false, mvLeft = false, mvRight = false;
457: let touchStartX = 0, touchStartY = 0;

473: const DOOR_LAYOUT = [
474:     { x: -3.0, z: -10, rot: Math.PI / 2 },
475:     { x: -3.0, z: -22, rot: Math.PI / 2 },
476:     { x: -3.0, z: -34, rot: Math.PI / 2 },
477:     { x: 3.0, z: -10, rot: -Math.PI / 2 },

479:     { x: 3.0, z: -34, rot: -Math.PI / 2 }

482: function init3D() {
483:     const holder = document.getElementById('canvas-holder');
484:     if (!holder) return;
485:     
486:     holder.innerHTML = '';
487:     
488:     // 1. WebGL рендерер
489:     renderer = new THREE.WebGLRenderer({ antialias: true, alpha: false });
490:     renderer.setSize(holder.clientWidth, holder.clientHeight);
491:     renderer.setPixelRatio(Math.min(window.devicePixelRatio, 2));
492:     renderer.shadowMap.enabled = true;
493:     holder.appendChild(renderer.domElement);
494:     
495:     // 2. Сцена и камера
496:     scene = new THREE.Scene();
497:     scene.background = new THREE.Color(0x060709);
498:     scene.fog = new THREE.FogExp2(0x060709, 0.06);
499:     
500:     camera = new THREE.PerspectiveCamera(65, holder.clientWidth / holder.clientHeight, 0.1, 100);
501:     camera.rotation.order = 'YXZ'; // Важно для FPS вращения!
502:     
503:     raycaster = new THREE.Raycaster();
504:     
505:     // 3. Создаем текстуры
506:     generateProceduralTextures();
507:     
508:     // 4. Строим сцену
509:     build3DScene();
510:     
511:     // Добавляем фонарик игрока (включен по умолчанию)
512:     playerFlashlight = new THREE.SpotLight(0xffffee, 2.5, 25, Math.PI / 3.5, 0.6, 1);
513:     playerFlashlight.position.set(0, 0, 0);
514:     playerFlashlight.target.position.set(0, 0, -1);
515:     playerFlashlight.castShadow = true;
516:     playerFlashlight.shadow.mapSize.width = 512;
517:     playerFlashlight.shadow.mapSize.height = 512;
518:     playerFlashlight.shadow.bias = -0.001;
519:     camera.add(playerFlashlight);
520:     camera.add(playerFlashlight.target);
521:     
522:     // Procedural First-Person Pistol Model
523:     const pistolGroup = new THREE.Group();
524:     pistolGroup.name = "pistol";
525:     const pistolMetal = new THREE.MeshStandardMaterial({ color: 0x22252a, roughness: 0.5, metalness: 0.8 });
526:     const pistolGrip = new THREE.MeshStandardMaterial({ color: 0x111215, roughness: 0.8 });
527:     const laserSightMat = new THREE.MeshBasicMaterial({ color: 0xff0000 });
528:     
529:     // Slide/Barrel
530:     const slideGeo = new THREE.BoxGeometry(0.04, 0.05, 0.22);
531:     const slide = new THREE.Mesh(slideGeo, pistolMetal);
532:     pistolGroup.add(slide);
533:     
534:     // Grip
535:     const gripGeo = new THREE.BoxGeometry(0.035, 0.11, 0.045);
536:     const grip = new THREE.Mesh(gripGeo, pistolGrip);
537:     grip.position.set(0, -0.065, 0.045);
538:     grip.rotation.x = 0.25;
539:     pistolGroup.add(grip);
540:     
541:     // Trigger guard
542:     const guardGeo = new THREE.BoxGeometry(0.015, 0.03, 0.05);
543:     const guard = new THREE.Mesh(guardGeo, pistolMetal);
544:     guard.position.set(0, -0.035, -0.02);
545:     pistolGroup.add(guard);
546: 
547:     // Laser module
548:     const laserGeo = new THREE.CylinderGeometry(0.007, 0.007, 0.07, 8);
549:     const laser = new THREE.Mesh(laserGeo, pistolMetal);
550:     laser.rotation.x = Math.PI / 2;
551:     laser.position.set(0, -0.022, -0.05);
552:     pistolGroup.add(laser);
553: 
554:     const lensGeo = new THREE.CylinderGeometry(0.004, 0.004, 0.002, 8);
555:     const lens = new THREE.Mesh(lensGeo, laserSightMat);
556:     lens.rotation.x = Math.PI / 2;
557:     lens.position.set(0, -0.022, -0.086);
558:     pistolGroup.add(lens);
559:     
560:     pistolGroup.position.set(0.18, -0.16, -0.32);
561:     pistolGroup.rotation.y = -Math.PI / 20;
562:     pistolGroup.rotation.x = Math.PI / 80;
563:     camera.add(pistolGroup);
564:     
565:     scene.add(camera);
566:     
567:     // 5. Запуск цикла
568:     animate3D();
569: }
570: 
571: function generateProceduralTextures() {
572:     const createNoiseTexture = (baseColor, noiseColor, scale = 1, isConcrete = false) => {
573:         const canvas = document.createElement('canvas');
574:         canvas.width = 256;
575:         canvas.height = 256;
576:         const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');
577:         
578:         ctx.fillStyle = baseColor;
579:         ctx.fillRect(0, 0, 256, 256);
580:         
581:         ctx.fillStyle = noiseColor;
582:         for (let i = 0; i < 6000; i++) {
583:             const size = Math.random() * 2 + 1;
584:             const x = Math.random() * 256;
585:             const y = Math.random() * 256;
586:             ctx.globalAlpha = Math.random() * 0.18;
587:             ctx.fillRect(x, y, size, size);
588:         }
589:         
590:         if (isConcrete) {
591:             ctx.fillStyle = 'rgba(74, 52, 32, 0.15)';
592:             for (let i = 0; i < 18; i++) {
593:                 ctx.beginPath();
594:                 ctx.arc(Math.random() * 256, Math.random() * 256, Math.random() * 60 + 20, 0, Math.PI * 2);
595:                 ctx.fill();
596:             }
597:             ctx.strokeStyle = 'rgba(10, 10, 10, 0.4)';
598:             ctx.lineWidth = 1;
599:             for (let i = 0; i < 4; i++) {
600:                 ctx.beginPath();
601:                 ctx.moveTo(Math.random() * 256, Math.random() * 256);
602:                 ctx.lineTo(Math.random() * 256, Math.random() * 256);
603:                 ctx.stroke();
604:             }
605:         }
606:         
607:         ctx.globalAlpha = 1.0;
608:         const tex = new THREE.CanvasTexture(canvas);
609:         tex.wrapS = THREE.RepeatWrapping;
610:         tex.wrapT = THREE.RepeatWrapping;
611:         tex.repeat.set(scale, scale);
612:         return tex;
613:     };
614:     
615:     const createDoorTexture = () => {
616:         const canvas = document.createElement('canvas');
617:         canvas.width = 256;
618:         canvas.height = 512;
619:         const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');
620:         
621:         ctx.fillStyle = '#2d333b';
622:         ctx.fillRect(0, 0, 256, 512);
623:         
624:         ctx.fillStyle = '#5c2d13';
625:         for (let i = 0; i < 2000; i++) {
626:             ctx.globalAlpha = Math.random() * 0.2;
627:             ctx.fillRect(Math.random() * 256, Math.random() * 512, Math.random() * 4 + 1, Math.random() * 4 + 1);
628:         }
629:         
630:         ctx.strokeStyle = '#111417';
631:         ctx.lineWidth = 4;
632:         ctx.strokeRect(10, 10, 236, 492);
633:         ctx.strokeRect(20, 20, 216, 230);
634:         ctx.strokeRect(20, 262, 216, 230);
635:         
636:         // Вентиль штурвала
637:         ctx.strokeStyle = '#4a5463';
638:         ctx.lineWidth = 8;
639:         ctx.beginPath();
640:         ctx.arc(128, 256, 42, 0, Math.PI * 2);
641:         ctx.stroke();
642:         
643:         ctx.fillStyle = '#111316';
644:         ctx.beginPath();
645:         ctx.arc(128, 256, 16, 0, Math.PI * 2);
646:         ctx.fill();
647:         
648:         ctx.fillStyle = '#4a5463';
649:         ctx.fillRect(124, 202, 8, 108);
650:         ctx.fillRect(74, 252, 108, 8);
651:         
652:         ctx.globalAlpha = 1.0;
653:         return new THREE.CanvasTexture(canvas);
654:     };
655: 
656:     const createWallpaperTexture = () => {
657:         const canvas = document.createElement('canvas');
658:         canvas.width = 128;

764:     // Procedural Normal Map generator from Canvas texture image data
765:     const createNormalMap = (srcCanvas, strength = 1.0) => {
766:         const width = srcCanvas.width;
767:         const height = srcCanvas.height;
768:         const ctxSrc = srcCanvas.getContext('2d');
769:         const imgData = ctxSrc.getImageData(0, 0, width, height);
770:         const data = imgData.data;
771: 
772:         const normalCanvas = document.createElement('canvas');
773:         normalCanvas.width = width;
774:         normalCanvas.height = height;
775:         const ctxDst = normalCanvas.getContext('2d');
776:         const dstData = ctxDst.createImageData(width, height);
777:         const dst = dstData.data;
778: 
779:         const getPixelIntensity = (x, y) => {
780:             const px = (x + width) % width;
781:             const py = (y + height) % height;
782:             const idx = (py * width + px) * 4;
783:             return (data[idx] + data[idx+1] + data[idx+2]) / 3;
784:         };
785: 
786:         for (let y = 0; y < height; y++) {
787:             for (let x = 0; x < width; x++) {
788:                 const idx = (y * width + x) * 4;
789:                 const hL = getPixelIntensity(x - 1, y);
790:                 const hR = getPixelIntensity(x + 1, y);
791:                 const hD = getPixelIntensity(x, y - 1);
792:                 const hU = getPixelIntensity(x, y + 1);
793:                 const dx = (hR - hL) / 255.0 * strength;
794:                 const dy = (hU - hD) / 255.0 * strength;
795:                 const len = Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy + 1.0);
796:                 const nx = -dx / len;
797:                 const ny = -dy / len;
798:                 const nz = 1.0 / len;
799:                 dst[idx]   = Math.floor((nx * 0.5 + 0.5) * 255);
800:                 dst[idx+1] = Math.floor((ny * 0.5 + 0.5) * 255);

The following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.
1: import re
2: import json
3: 
4: with open('app.js', encoding='utf-8') as f:
5:     content = f.read()
6: 
7: # Extract LANGUAGES['ru']
8: # We'll use a regex to grab the inside of "ru": { ... }
9: ru_match = re.search(r'"ru":\s*({.*?\n\s*})', content, re.DOTALL)
10: if ru_match:
11:     ru_json_str = ru_match.group(1)
12:     # The JSON string has some trailing commas or comments maybe?
13:     # Let's try to parse it with a simple parser or eval.
14:     # Actually, it's valid JS object, not necessarily strict JSON (keys might not be quoted, though they probably are).
15:     # Since I wrote it, keys are quoted.
16:     try:
17:         # In python, json.loads needs strict JSON.
18:         ru_dict = json.loads(ru_json_str)
19:     except:
20:         # fallback: manual parse
21:         ru_dict = {}
22:         for line in ru_json_str.split('\n'):
23:             m = re.search(r'"([^"]+)":\s*"([^"]+)"', line)
24:             if m:
25:                 ru_dict[m.group(1)] = m.group(2)
26: else:
27:     print("Could not find ru dict")
28:     exit(1)
29: 
30: # Now find all t('key') or t('key', args) in content
31: def replace_t(match):
32:     key = match.group(1)
33:     args = match.group(2)
34:     val = ru_dict.get(key, key)
35:     
36:     if args:
37:         # args is something like `, num` or `, door.sector, door.sectorNum`
38:         # We need to construct a JS template string or concat if there are {0}, {1} in val.
39:         # However, it's easier to just do it in JS, or we replace {0} with `${num}` etc.
40:         # Let's check which strings have {0}
41:         args_list = [a.strip() for a in args.split(',') if a.strip()]
42:         for i, a in enumerate(args_list):
43:             val = val.replace(f"{{{i}}}", f"${{{a}}}")
44:         return f"`{val}`"
45:     else:
46:         return f"'{val}'"
47: 
48: # Replace t('key', ...)
49: # t\('([^']+)'(?:,\s*([^)]+))?\)
50: new_content = re.sub(r"t\('([^']+)'(?:,\s*([^)]+))?\)", replace_t, content)
51: 
52: # Remove LANGUAGES block
53: # It starts at const LANGUAGES = { and ends with }; before function t(
54: new_content = re.sub(r'const\s+LANGUAGES\s*=\s*\{.*?\n};\n+', '', new_content, flags=re.DOTALL)
55: 
56: # Remove function t(key, ...args) { ... }
57: new_content = re.sub(r'function\s+t\(key,\s*\.\.\.args\)\s*\{.*?\n\}\n+', '', new_content, flags=re.DOTALL)
58: 
59: with open('app.js', 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
60:     f.write(new_content)
61: 
62: print("Translation logic removed successfully.")
63: 
The above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.

764:     // Procedural Normal Map generator from Canvas texture image data
765:     const createNormalMap = (srcCanvas, strength = 1.0) => {
766:         const width = srcCanvas.width;
767:         const height = srcCanvas.height;
768:         const ctxSrc = srcCanvas.getContext('2d');
769:         const imgData = ctxSrc.getImageData(0, 0, width, height);
770:         const data = imgData.data;
771: 
772:         const normalCanvas = document.createElement('canvas');
773:         normalCanvas.width = width;
774:         normalCanvas.height = height;
775:         const ctxDst = normalCanvas.getContext('2d');
776:         const dstData = ctxDst.createImageData(width, height);
777:         const dst = dstData.data;
778: 
779:         const getPixelIntensity = (x, y) => {
780:             const px = (x + width) % width;
781:             const py = (y + height) % height;
782:             const idx = (py * width + px) * 4;
783:             return (data[idx] + data[idx+1] + data[idx+2]) / 3;
784:         };
785: 
786:         for (let y = 0; y < height; y++) {
787:             for (let x = 0; x < width; x++) {
788:                 const idx = (y * width + x) * 4;
789:                 const hL = getPixelIntensity(x - 1, y);
790:                 const hR = getPixelIntensity(x + 1, y);
791:                 const hD = getPixelIntensity(x, y - 1);
792:                 const hU = getPixelIntensity(x, y + 1);
793:                 const dx = (hR - hL) / 255.0 * strength;
794:                 const dy = (hU - hD) / 255.0 * strength;
795:                 const len = Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy + 1.0);
796:                 const nx = -dx / len;
797:                 const ny = -dy / len;
798:                 const nz = 1.0 / len;
799:                 dst[idx]   = Math.floor((nx * 0.5 + 0.5) * 255);
800:                 dst[idx+1] = Math.floor((ny * 0.5 + 0.5) * 255);

**Analyzing Collision Physics**

I'm currently focused on refining the collision mechanics, particularly concerning player-wall interactions and camera clipping. I've re-examined the initial calculations for collision distances, and the impact of the player's position relative to walls. There are questions arising about room wall geometry and the camera's near clipping plane, aiming to address the visual artifacts near walls, specifically when the player rotates. I am now looking at the boundary conditions that are applied on stairs.


**Investigating Collision Boundaries**

I've discovered a critical flaw in the stair collision handling! The code allows the player to move directly against the wall's inner surface, leading to severe camera clipping and visual artifacts. Specifically, the left wall (`shaftL`) permits a proximity of only 0.1 units before the camera near plane penetrates, while the back wall (`shaftB`) allows the player's center to be *inside* the wall. This issue demands immediate attention.


**Refining Boundary Conditions**

I'm now zeroing in on the staircase boundary issues, specifically the problematic proximity to the walls. The current checks allow the camera to penetrate walls due to insufficient buffer distance, especially around the right wall (`shaftR`) and the partition. To fix this, I'm adjusting the hardcoded boundary conditions to account for the player's radius. The left wall's `nextX` needs to be `>= -1.2`, the right wall's `<= 1.2`, and now I'm calculating for the partition as well.

5154: 
5155: function playSoundMask() {
5156:     if (!audioCtx) return;
5157:     try {
5158:         const now = audioCtx.currentTime;
5159:         const bufferSize = audioCtx.sampleRate * 0.8;
5160:         const buffer = audioCtx.createBuffer(1, bufferSize, audioCtx.sampleRate);
5161:         const data = buffer.getChannelData(0);
5162:         for (let i = 0; i < bufferSize; i++) {
5163:             data[i] = Math.random() * 2 - 1;
5164:         }
5165:         const noise = audioCtx.createBufferSource();
5166:         noise.buffer = buffer;
5167:         
5168:         const filter = audioCtx.createBiquadFilter();
5169:         filter.type = 'bandpass';
5170:         filter.frequency.setValueAtTime(200, now);
5171:         filter.frequency.linearRampToValueAtTime(600, now + 0.4);
5172:         filter.frequency.linearRampToValueAtTime(150, now + 0.8);
5173:         
5174:         const gain = audioCtx.createGain();
5175:         gain.gain.setValueAtTime(0.08, now);
5176:         gain.gain.exponentialRampToValueAtTime(0.001, now + 0.8);
5177:         
5178:         noise.connect(filter);
5179:         filter.connect(gain);
5180:         gain.connect(audioCtx.destination);
5181:         
5182:         noise.start(now);
5183:         noise.stop(now + 0.8);
5184:     } catch (e) {}
5185: }
5186: 
5187: function playSoundDrink() {
5188:     if (!audioCtx) return;
5189:     try {
5190:         const now = audioCtx.currentTime;
5191:         for (let i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
5192:             const time = now + i * 0.22;
5193:             const osc = audioCtx.createOscillator();
5194:             const gain = audioCtx.createGain();
5195:             
5196:             osc.type = 'sine';
5197:             osc.frequency.setValueAtTime(220, time);
5198:             osc.frequency.exponentialRampToValueAtTime(120, time + 0.12);
5199:             
5200:             gain.gain.setValueAtTime(0.12, time);
5201:             gain.gain.exponentialRampToValueAtTime(0.001, time + 0.12);
5202:             
5203:             osc.connect(gain);
5204:             gain.connect(audioCtx.destination);
5205:             
5206:             osc.start(time);
5207:             osc.stop(time + 0.18);
5208:         }
5209:     } catch (e) {}
5210: }

31: function getLocalizedDoorName(door) {
32:     if (!door) return "";
33:     if (door.type === 'apartment') {
34:         const num = door.number || 101;
35:         if (door.roomType === 'armory') {
36:             return `Пост Ликвидаторов №${num}`;
37:         } else if (door.roomType === 'contaminated') {
38:             return `Квартира ${num} (РАДИАЦИЯ)`;
39:         } else if (door.roomType === 'nest') {
40:             return `Техническая секция ${num} (ГНЕЗДО)`;
41:         } else {
42:             return `Квартира ${num}`;
43:         }
44:     } else if (door.type === 'transition') {
45:         return `Переход ${door.sector || 'А'}-${door.sectorNum || 1}`;
46:     } else if (door.type === 'monster') {
47:         const keys = ['room_warehouse', 'room_archive', 'room_compressor', 'room_control', 'room_ventilation'];
48:         const key = keys[door.monsterRoomIndex] || 'room_warehouse';
49:         return t(key);
50:     } else if (door.type === 'empty') {
51:         const idx = door.id !== undefined ? (door.id + 1) : 1;
52:         return `Дверь ${idx}`;
53:     }
54:     return door.name || "";
55: }

1: # Goal Description
2: Make the "True Ending" (Awakened) main menu alive. Specifically:
3: - Implement a day and night cycle.
4: - Add trees that grow over time.
5: - Add children that occasionally come to play.
6: 
7: ## Proposed Changes
8: 
9: ### `app.js`
10: The current implementation of the "true ending" main menu background relies on the `animateMenuNature()` function, which draws a static daytime scene (sky, pulsating sun, clouds, particles) onto a 2D canvas overlay.
11: 
12: I will completely rewrite `animateMenuNature()` to incorporate stateful animation logic:
13: 
14: #### 1. Day/Night Cycle
15: - Introduce a `cyclePhase` variable that loops continuously (e.g., a 20-second day/night cycle).
16: - Use this variable to interpolate sky gradient colors:
17:   - **Noon**: Bright blue sky (`#7ec0ee`, `#e0f6ff`).
18:   - **Sunset**: Orange/purple sky.
19:   - **Night**: Dark blue/black sky with stars.
20:   - **Sunrise**: Pink/orange sky.
21: - Animate a Sun traversing the sky during the day, and a Moon during the night.
22: 
23: #### 2. Growing Trees
24: - Define a `trees` array.
25: - Each tree will have a randomized position, scale limit, and current `age`.
26: - As time progresses, the `age` will increase, and the tree's trunk and canopy will be rendered larger, simulating growth.
27: - The trees will be drawn as stylized 2D vector shapes (brown trunk, green overlapping circles for leaves) to match the abstract "pixel/surreal" nature of the game.
28: 
29: #### 3. Children Playing
30: - Define a `children` array.
31: - Occasionally spawn a "child" object off-screen.
32: - The child will run across the screen (using a simple walking/jumping sine-wave animation on the Y-axis) and disappear when it reaches the other side.
33: - They will be drawn as simple silhouettes or abstract colorful figures (to fit the surreal awakening theme).
34: 
35: ## Verification Plan
36: ### Manual Verification
37: - After implementation, I will manually open the `index.html` file using the browser to test the main menu (triggering the awakened achievement if necessary via console).
38: - Ensure the day/night transitions smoothly without flickering.
39: - Verify that trees grow visibly over time.
40: - Verify that child silhouettes spawn and animate across the screen.
41: 
42: > [!IMPORTANT]
43: > The current main menu overlay has a `pointerEvents = 'none'` rule, so the 2D canvas does not block UI buttons. This will remain unchanged.
44: 
45: ## Open Questions
46: - Do you have a preferred duration for the day/night cycle (e.g., fast like 15 seconds, or slower like 60 seconds)? I plan to default to 20 seconds for visual dynamism.
47: - Do you want the trees to grow forever or stop at a maximum size? I plan to make them stop at a randomized max size.

**Analyzing Spatial Gaps**

I've zeroed in on the staircase issue. I was re-reading critical instructions 1 and 2, which led me to analyze the spatial dimensions of the staircase. I realized there is no ceiling above the staircase hole, which aligns perfectly with the feedback about "emptiness" on the stairs. This explains why the player sees void when looking up, and also the lamp floating mid-air! To fix this, I need to add a ceiling to the staircase shaft.


**Calculating Staircase Ceiling**

I'm now calculating the exact height for the staircase ceiling. I've determined the staircase walls are 5 units high, centered at `baseY - 2.5`, thus ranging from `baseY - 5.0` to `baseY`. I've confirmed the next floor's floor is at `baseY + 5.0`, which should be the ceiling height. I'm verifying the current floor's stairs go from `baseY` down to `baseY - 5.0`. There could be a rendering issue and gap here. I'm investigating how `buildFloor` generates corridor and staircase walls, specifically their heights and positions.

7: > [!IMPORTANT]
8: > - **Staircase ceiling & walls extension**: We are enclosing the staircase shaft to prevent seeing the black void. This will make the stairs look like a fully built concrete stairwell.
9: > - **Valve wheel centration**: We are moving the door valve wheel to the center of the door and doubling its size (0.24 radius instead of 0.12) to match standard submarine/bunker hatch aesthetics.
10: > - **WebGL performance optimization**: We will pre-create and cache all standard Three.js materials (`wallMat`, `floorMat`, `ceilingMat`, etc.) and the muzzle flash point light. This will completely eliminate performance drops during shooting and floor transitions.
11: 
12: ## Proposed Changes
13: 
14: ### 3D Engine & Collision (`app.js`)
15: 
16: #### [MODIFY] [app.js](file:///C:/Users/m3615/samosbor_game/app.js)
17: 
18: ##### 1. Camera Clipping
19: - Change the near clipping plane of the `THREE.PerspectiveCamera` in `init3D` from `0.1` to `0.02`. This allows the player to get extremely close to walls and doors without clipping through them.
20: 
21: ##### 2. Material Caching & Performance Optimizations
22: - Introduce a global `cachedMaterials` object.
23: - Initialize all standard game materials inside `generateProceduralTextures()` or `init3D()` once using cached texture
<truncated 2866 bytes>

=== STEP 4748 (PLANNER_RESPONSE) ===
THINKING:
**Analyzing the Shooting Function**

I've been looking closely at the 'shoot' function. It appears to utilize a Three.js point light for a visual "flash" effect. The light's color is set to `0xfff0c0`, intensity to 4.0, and range to 30. The flash is positioned relative to the camera, which is expected.


**Optimizing the Flash Implementation**

I've realized creating a new light object on every shot causes performance issues. I'm pre-creating the light (with zero intensity), now named `shootFlashLight`, to avoid shader recompilation.  It will be either attached to the camera or the scene. I'm setting the intensity to 4.0 when shooting and back to 0 after 60ms. Moreover, I'm adjusting the near clipping plane on the camera to 0.02. This should fix the wall clipping issue.

=== STEP 4812 (PLANNER_RESPONSE) ===
THINKING:
**Designing the Menu's Features**

I'm excited! The initial rendering to `menuNatureCanvas` using `animateMenuNature()` is a great starting point. I'm focused now on developing the "Living Main Menu" features. First up is the day/night cycle. My current idea is a looping cycle of 40-60 seconds.


**Defining the Time-Based Phases**

I've specified the sky gradients and ambient light for four phases: Day, Sunset, Night, and Sunrise, each spanning 10 seconds in a 40-second loop. The gradient interpolations are planned, leveraging a `cycleTime` computation to determine the current phase. The plan is to create variables to handle colors, intensity, offsets, and a boolean for `isNight`. This will make the day/night cycle dynamic!


**Implementing the Cycle Logic**

I'm now integrating the phase logic using lerpColor for smooth transitions. I'm focusing on defining the colors, intensity, and offsets for the sun and moon, along with `isNight`. The day/night cycle will be based on a computed `cycleTime`. I have created helper function `lerpColor()` for color interpolation.


**Refining the Color Parsing**

I've just added a very clean function, `lerpColor()`, to interpolate between colors. And the associated hex parser is working flawlessly now. I'm focusing now on the growing trees feature! I've also come up with a `menuTrees` array that stores data on each tree's X-ratio position, growth, speed, sway, and maxScale. The goal is to grow a dynamic forest.


**Growing the Trees**

I'm now focused on making the trees grow dynamically! The plan is for them to sprout from seedlings to full size in 40-60 seconds. I've added code to increase their age in the animation loop and a helper function, `drawTree()`, for rendering the trees, including their trunk and foliage. This should result in a dynamic growing forest!


**Calculating the Hill Geometry**

I've just derived the exact Y-coordinate for the grassy hill at any given X-coordinate using an ellipse equation. The `getHillY()` function accurately calculates the height based on the ellipse's center, radii, and a given x-position. I'm satisfied; it's mathematically precise! Now, I am thinking about adding children who are playing!



**Adding Playful Children**

I'm now implementing the `menuChildren` array, focusing on parameters like initial position, direction, speed, and behavior (`'walk'`, `'run'`, `'skip'`). I've created a child object with varied parameters. In the animation loop, I update each child's position, calculate the bounce effect on the hill, and handle screen boundary conditions, removing children who leave the screen. I'm excited about the dynamic interaction!


**Adding the Children's Animation**

I've crafted an elegant drawing function for the children, complete with animated legs and arms, capturing their movement as they traverse the grassy hill. The animation includes varying behaviors: walking, running, and bouncing along the hill's precise mathematical curve. Additionally, during the night phase, I've incorporated darker child silhouettes and reduced their frequency. The night sky is set to be enriched with twinkling stars!


**Adding the Twinkling Stars**

I'm working on adding visual richness to the night sky! I'm now implementing a twinkling star effect using `sin()` for subtle, randomized light variations. I'm also including a glowing moon with a subtle shadow to create a premium, atmospheric, and visually stunning effect. I'm thrilled with how it's shaping up. The user will be wowed!


**Adjusting Loot Probabilities**

I've just recalibrated the loot distribution. The goal is now a 60% chance of finding something useful. I've re-weighted the probabilities to reflect this, incorporating the `bandage` as a new loot option. I'm focusing on ensuring its functionality within the inventory system. I'm excited about this enhancement!

31: function getLocalizedDoorName(door) {
32:     if (!door) return "";
33:     if (door.type === 'apartment') {
34:         const num = door.number || 101;
35:         if (door.roomType === 'armory') {
36:             return `Пост Ликвидаторов №${num}`;
37:         } else if (door.roomType === 'contaminated') {
38:             return `Квартира ${num} (РАДИАЦИЯ)`;
39:         } else if (door.roomType === 'nest') {
40:             return `Техническая секция ${num} (ГНЕЗДО)`;
41:         } else {
42:             return `Квартира ${num}`;
43:         }
44:     } else if (door.type === 'transition') {
45:         return `Переход ${door.sector || 'А'}-${door.sectorNum || 1}`;
46:     } else if (door.type === 'monster') {
47:         const keys = ['room_warehouse', 'room_archive', 'room_compressor', 'room_control', 'room_ventilation'];
48:         const key = keys[door.monsterRoomIndex] || 'room_warehouse';
49:         return t(key);
50:     } else if (door.type === 'empty') {
51:         const idx = door.id !== undefined ? (door.id + 1) : 1;
52:         return `Дверь ${idx}`;
53:     }
54:     return door.name || "";
55: }
